nodes_flat.title: 5.14. Security measures
nodes_flat.summary: Imagine Montessori School prioritizes your data security. For offline data collection, we obtain your explicit consent via a signature on a paper document. We've conducted an exhaustive risk analysis of all data processing to protect your fundamental rights. We implement the strictest security levels for our website and services, safeguarding data integrity and preventing unauthorized access. We use our own technology and partner only with reputable third-party suppliers who meet legal security regulations, ensuring your information is safe from loss, misuse, or unauthorized access.
nodes_flat.content:
When we collect information from the user by another means, for example, a written form, we will obtain the user's consent to the processing by means of a signature on a paper document.

The entity has carried out an exhaustive risk analysis of all the data processing carried out. As a result of this analysis, it has defined each process and tried to minimise any possible risks, guaranteeing with the utmost diligence the adequate protection of the Fundamental Rights of users within the entity's environment.

The entity is aware of the user's concern for security and has therefore chosen to implement the strictest levels of security that allow the proper functioning of the website, its functionalities and its services, preserving the integrity of the data and information stored. The services are provided with its own technological resources and sometimes by third parties who have the necessary security measures to ensure the impossibility of unauthorised access according to the current ordinary technique and technology. At Imagine Montessori School we want the service that our clients receive to offer a satisfactory and secure user experience, which is why we only choose suppliers with a reputable track record and technical solvency that allow compliance with current legal security regulations, to prevent loss, misuse or access by unauthorised persons.

nodes_flat.title: 5.15. Legal information
nodes_flat.summary: Our privacy rules add to, but don't replace, existing data protection laws; those laws always come first. We regularly review and can update these rules anytime. If we make changes, we'll tell you, and you'll need to read the newest version and confirm you agree to it.
nodes_flat.content:
The requirements of this Policy supplement, and do not replace, any other requirements existing under applicable data protection law, which shall prevail in any event.

This Policy is subject to periodic review and may be amended by us at any time. When this happens, we will notify you of any changes and ask you to re-read the most recent version of our Policy and confirm your acceptance of it.

nodes_flat.title: 5.17. Reference Documents
nodes_flat.summary: These documents outline how your child's personal data is protected. We adhere to EU law (GDPR 2016/679) and Spanish law (LOPDGDD 3/2018). ZUBI EDUCATION and ZUBI GROUP IMPACT have internal policies for collecting, storing, processing, and safeguarding data, including security and privacy. The Spanish Data Protection Agency (AEPD) oversees compliance.

*   Ley Orgánica 3/2018, de 5 de diciembre, de Protección de Datos Personales y Garantía de los Derechos Digitales (LOPDGDD): This is the Spanish data protection law that complements the GDPR and ensures the protection of personal data within Spain.

*   The Spanish Data Protection Agency (AEPD): The official agency responsible for monitoring data protection compliance in Spain and providing guidance on data protection matters.

nodes_flat.title: 6. Whistleblowing Channel Policy
nodes_flat.summary:
Honey, Zubi Education, SL has a 'Whistleblowing Channel' for serious issues, not just complaints. Anyone connected – staff, clients, suppliers – *must* report serious breaches like fraud, harassment, or safety risks. It's secure, confidential, and you're protected from retaliation.

You can report anonymously via the website's 'canal de denuncias'. A 'System Administrator' manages it. They acknowledge reports within 7 days, and you get a response within 3 months (can extend). If it's a crime, it goes to the Public Prosecutor.

Your identity as an 'Informant' is *never* revealed to the 'Affected Individual'. False or malicious reports are serious and lead to disciplinary action. This policy is reviewed biennially and follows EU Directive 2019/1937 and Spanish Law 2/2023 for protection.

nodes_flat.title: 6.1. Scope
nodes_flat.summary: The Whistleblowing Channel is for employees, clients, suppliers, and third parties. All employees (any employment type), suppliers, sponsors, collaborators, agents, and company board members *must* report any serious irregularity or breach they encounter. They are safe from dismissal or retaliation, and reports are kept confidential. This Channel is strictly for reporting these specific issues, not for general complaints.
nodes_flat.content:
This policy is intended for employees, clients, suppliers, and third parties involved in the Company's operations, who may use the Whistleblowing Channel as detailed herein.

All employees (regardless of the legal form of their employment or service relationship), suppliers, sponsors, collaborators, agents, and members of the Company's various collegiate bodies are required to report any irregularity or breach within their objective scope through the Whistleblowing Channel, without fear of dismissal or other forms of retaliation. The information provided will be treated with utmost confidentiality.

The Whistleblowing Channel is intended as an instrument for reporting irregularities or breaches. Therefore, it should not be used indiscriminately but solely for the purposes for which it is designed.

nodes_flat.title: 6.2. Terminology
nodes_flat.summary: Key terms define how concerns are reported. A **Whistleblower** (anyone connected to the School) uses the secure **Whistleblowing Channel** on our website to submit a **Report** about potential breaches or irregularities. The **System Administrator** manages this channel. An **Investigation Committee** oversees investigations. The person reporting is an **Informant**, while those implicated are **Affected Individuals**. An **Ad Hoc Team** handles specific cases, especially for conflicts of interest.
nodes_flat.content:
*   **Whistleblower**: An individual who reports, or provides information concerning, a potential breach, offence, or irregularity in the operations of the Company, including employees, clients, suppliers, partners, or any other relevant third parties.
*   **Whistleblowing Channel**: Our platform and communication system made available in our website to all individuals connected to our School, allowing for the confidential and secure reporting of any breach or irregularity as defined in this policy.
*   **Report**: Any communication made through the Whistleblowing Channel, providing information about a potential breach, offence, or irregularity that may require investigation.
*   **System Administrator**: The individual responsible for the operation and management of the Whistleblowing Channel, including the receipt, processing, and registration of reports.
*   **Investigation Committee**: A designated group responsible for overseeing the investigation of the reported facts, ensuring compliance with the internal procedures and regulations.
*   **Informant**: The person submitting a report through the Whistleblowing Channel.
*   **Affected Individual**: Any person or entity that is implicated or involved in a report submitted through the Whistleblowing Channel.
*   **Ad Hoc Team**: A specific group designated to handle particular reports, based on the subject matter or potential conflict of interest of the System Administrator.

nodes_flat.title: 6.3. Policy Principles and Statements
nodes_flat.summary: Zubi Education, SL ("the Company") established a "Whistleblowing Channel" to ensure proper conduct. It complies with EU Directive 2019/1937, protecting those who report issues. Employees, clients, suppliers, or third parties can use it to report serious breaches, like violations of Anti-Money Laundering, Data Protection, or equality regulations, or the Internal Code of Conduct. Accessible via their corporate website, it offers secure, confidential, and anonymous reporting for potential administrative or criminal risks.
nodes_flat.content:
This policy has been developed by Zubi Education, SL (hereinafter referred to as "the Company"), to maintain group-wide consistency of action. Its objective is to outline the procedure followed within the internal whistleblowing channel, in compliance with various regulatory provisions, including Article 8 of Directive (EU) 2019/1937 of the European Parliament and Council dated 23 October 2019, regarding the protection of individuals who report breaches of EU law.

The objective of the Whistleblowing Channel is to enable employees, clients, suppliers, and third parties associated with the Company or its operations to report any incident within the Company that could represent a breach, offence, or violation of our internal procedures, among others:

*   Breach of Anti-Money Laundering (AML) regulations
*   Breach of Data Protection regulations
*   Breach of Market Abuse regulations
*   Breach of comprehensive sexual freedom assurance regulations
*   Breach of regulations ensuring effective equality between men and women
*   Internal Code of Conduct

The Company’s Whistleblowing Channel is accessible through the corporate website: , and is available to individuals aware of potential administrative and/or serious criminal risks attributable to any person or entity linked to the group in their professional capacity.

This Channel enables secure communication, ensuring that privacy and confidentiality are maintained throughout the process. Reports may be submitted anonymously if the whistleblower so chooses.

nodes_flat.title: 6.4. General Responsibilities
nodes_flat.summary: The Board approves the Whistleblowing Policy and resources. The Control Unit updates it. The System Administrator operates the channel, ensures whistleblower confidentiality, records complaints, and manages documentation. Specialized officers (AML, Code of Conduct, Data Protection, Equal Opportunities) support investigations into breaches like money laundering, company rules, data privacy, and equality issues.
nodes_flat.content:
The general responsibilities regarding the Whistleblowing Channel are outlined below:

**Board of Directors:**

*   Approval of this Policy and its subsequent updates.
*   Provision of the necessary resources for the proper functioning of the Channel.

**Control Unit:**

*   Updating of this policy.

**System Administrator:**

*   Operation of the whistleblowing channel.
*   Ensuring confidentiality of whistleblowers, when applicable.
*   Recording complaints.
*   Preservation of documentation.
*   Providing information to the investigative committee in cases of complaints of workplace and/or sexual harassment.

**Internal Control Body for Anti-Money Laundering (AML) Compliance:**

*   Support for the Criminal Risk Prevention Body in complaints related to breaches of AML/CTF law.

**Internal Code of Conduct Monitoring Body:**

*   Support for the Criminal Risk Prevention Body in complaints regarding breaches of the Company's Internal Code of Conduct.
*   Support for the Criminal Risk Prevention Body in complaints related to breaches of Market Abuse regulations.

**Data Protection Security Officer:**

*   Support for the Criminal Risk Prevention Body in complaints related to breaches of data protection regulations.

**Equal Opportunities Officer:**

*   Support for the Body responsible for compliance with equality regulations between men and women, and for ensuring comprehensive sexual freedom.

nodes_flat.title: 6.5. Means of Communication
nodes_flat.summary: For reporting concerns, the website's "canal de denuncias" (whistleblowing channel) is the preferred method. It guarantees confidential data processing, as required by the Data Protection Act, ensuring your information is handled privately and securely.
nodes_flat.content: The public whistleblowing channel (“canal de denuncias”) on our website should be used as the preferred method for submitting information, as it ensures confidential data processing in accordance with the Data Protection Act.

nodes_flat.title: 6.6. Processing of Reports
nodes_flat.summary: Our "Whistleblower Channel" on the website lets anyone connected to us (like clients, staff, or partners) confidentially report concerns about bad behavior. We protect everyone's privacy and assume innocence. Investigations usually get a response within 3 months of the report (or 3 months plus 7 days if no receipt confirmation). For tricky cases, this can extend by another 3 months. We might ask the person who reported for more information. If it's a crime, we send it straight to the Public Prosecutor, or the European Public Prosecutor if EU money is involved.
nodes_flat.content:
The "Whistleblower Channel" is available on our website and is accessible to all individuals who have a relationship with us, including clients, employees, suppliers, partners, or any other parties connected to our activities. This channel allows the reporting of irregularities or inappropriate behavior, ensuring confidentiality and the right to the presumption of innocence for all involved parties. The information received through this channel will be handled with the utmost seriousness and in compliance with personal data protection regulations, ensuring that investigation procedures are conducted in accordance with applicable laws.

As a general rule, the maximum time to respond to investigation actions shall not exceed three months from the receipt of the report or, if no acknowledgment of receipt was sent to the informant, three months from the expiration of the seven-day period following the submission of the report, except in cases of special complexity that require an extension of the period, in which case it may be extended by a maximum of an additional three months.

At various stages of the processing procedure, there is provision for maintaining communication with the informant and, if deemed necessary, requesting additional information from the informant, unless they have waived this option.

In all cases, the right to the presumption of innocence and the honour of the affected parties shall be respected, as well as the provisions on the protection of personal data as stipulated.

If the reported facts are potentially indicative of a crime, the information will be referred immediately to the Public Prosecutor’s Office. Should the facts affect the financial interests of the European Union, they will be referred to the European Public Prosecutor’s Office.

nodes_flat.title: 6.7. Receipt and Registration of the Report
nodes_flat.summary: You can report concerns anonymously or confidentially. You decide if you want updates or not, and can review/sign your report. Each case in the whistleblowing channel gets an ID code and is stored securely. We record the date, ID, actions, measures, and closure. An acknowledgment is sent within 7 days, unless you declined updates or it risks confidentiality.
nodes_flat.content:
Information can be provided anonymously. In other cases, the identity of the informant will be kept confidential.

When submitting information, the informant may provide an address, email, or secure location for receiving notifications and may also expressly decline to receive any communications regarding actions taken by the Company because of the report.

Without prejudice to the rights accorded to them under data protection regulations, the informant will be given the opportunity to review, correct, and sign the transcript of the message.

Each case will be recorded within the whistleblowing channel and assigned an identification code. All information recorded within the whistleblowing channel will be stored in a secure, restricted-access database, where the following details of each communication will be registered:

a)  Date of receipt.
b)  Identification code.
c)  Actions taken.
d)  Measures adopted.
e)  Closure date.

Upon receipt of the information, an acknowledgment will be issued within a maximum period of 7 calendar days from receipt, unless the informant has expressly declined to receive communications related to the investigation, or unless doing so could jeopardise the confidentiality of the report.

nodes_flat.title: 6.8. Admission Process
nodes_flat.summary:
The System Administrator verifies reports against Annex I, reassigning if a conflict of interest exists or an ad hoc team (Chief Officer People, Zubi Group) is responsible. Within 10 business days, a decision is made:
a) Dismiss the report if implausible, not an Annex I breach, unfounded (crime-obtained facts sent to Public Prosecutor’s Office), or lacking new info. Informant notified within 5 business days.
b) Admit for processing. Informant notified within 7 business days (unless waived).
c) Forward to Public Prosecutor’s Office (if crime) or European Public Prosecutor’s Office (if EU financial interests).
d) Refer to the competent authority.
nodes_flat.content:
Once the information is registered, the System Administrator will verify whether it presents facts or behaviours that fall within the scope of Annex I. They will also determine if the report needs to be reassigned to another person or management team due to any of the following circumstances:

1.  Conflict of interest of the System Administrator, as they may be personally affected by the report.
2.  Existence of an ad hoc team responsible for managing the report based on the subject matter.

The ad hoc team for handling reports will, in this case, be composed of the Chief Officer People of Zubi Group, ensuring impartiality in the process.

Following this preliminary analysis, a decision will be made within a maximum period of ten business days from the registration date of the information:

a)  **Dismiss the report** in any of the following cases:
    1.  When the facts reported are entirely implausible.
    2.  When the facts reported do not constitute a breach of the legal framework covered under Annex I.
    3.  When the report is manifestly unfounded, or there are, in the System Administrator’s opinion, reasonable indications that it was obtained through the commission of a crime. In this latter case, in addition to dismissal, a detailed report of the facts deemed to constitute a crime will be forwarded to the Public Prosecutor’s Office.
    4.  When the report contains no new and significant information regarding breaches in comparison with a previous report for which the procedures have been concluded, unless there are new factual or legal circumstances that justify a different follow-up. In such cases, the System Administrator will issue a reasoned notification of the decision.
    The dismissal will be communicated to the informant within five business days.

b)  **Admit the report for processing**.
    The admission of the report will be communicated to the informant within seven business days, unless the informant has waived the right to receive communications from the Company’s System Administrator.

c)  **Immediately forward the information to the Public Prosecutor’s Office** if the facts may indicate a crime, or to the European Public Prosecutor’s Office if the facts affect the financial interests of the European Union.

d)  **Refer the report** to the competent authority, entity, or body for processing.

nodes_flat.title: 6.9. Investigation
nodes_flat.summary: The investigation verifies reported facts. The "affected individual" (respondent) is informed of the report, summarized facts, their right to submit written arguments, and personal data handling. This disclosure can be delayed to the "hearing stage" if it risks evidence concealment or destruction. The "informant's" identity is never revealed, nor do affected parties access the full report. Respondents receive a facts summary, can be interviewed to present their version and evidence, and access the "case file" (without informant details), respecting "presumption of innocence". The "System Administrator" ensures confidentiality. All Company personnel must cooperate, providing requested documentation or data.
nodes_flat.content:
The investigation will encompass all actions aimed at verifying the credibility of the reported facts.

It will be ensured that the person affected by the information is informed of the report, as well as of the facts presented in a summarised form. Additionally, they will be informed of their right to submit written arguments and the handling of their personal data. However, this information may be provided at the hearing stage if prior disclosure could facilitate the concealment, destruction, or alteration of evidence.

In no case will the identity of the informant be disclosed to the affected parties, nor will they be given access to the report. During the investigation, a summary of the reported facts will be provided to the respondent. This information may also be presented at the hearing if prior disclosure could facilitate the concealment, destruction, or alteration of evidence.

Without prejudice to the right to submit written arguments, the investigation will include, whenever possible, an interview with the affected individual in which they are invited to present their version of the events and to provide any evidence they consider appropriate and relevant, all while respecting the presumption of innocence.

To ensure the right to a defence, the affected individual will have access to the case file without revealing any information that could identify the informant, and they may be heard at any point in the proceedings.

The System Administrator is obligated to maintain confidentiality regarding the information they acquire during this investigation.

All Company personnel are required to cooperate with the investigation and must comply with any requests made to provide documentation, data, or any information related to the ongoing procedures, including personal data if required.

nodes_flat.title: 6.10. Conclusion of Proceedings
nodes_flat.summary: After an investigation, the System Administrator decides what happens next. Options include: closing the case (notifying the person who reported it and the affected individual, keeping their protection unless the report was clearly wrong), sending it to the Public Prosecutor (or European Public Prosecutor if EU money is involved), passing it to another authority, or starting disciplinary action. A private record is kept in the whistleblowing software, noting when it was received, its type, when it closed, and what was done. This record isn't public; only a judge can ask to see it. Personal data is kept for up to 10 years. If wrongdoing is proven, employees face sanctions (from their contract, labor laws, etc.) and the company might tell the police. If it's a supplier or partner, they face commercial penalties like contract termination, and the police might still be informed.
nodes_flat.content:
At the conclusion of the investigation, the System Administrator will adopt one of the following decisions:

a)  **Filing of the case**, which will be notified to the informant and, if applicable, to the affected individual. In such cases, the informant will retain the right to protection as provided by this law, unless, because of the actions carried out during the investigation phase, it is concluded that the report should have been dismissed based on one of the grounds specified in this Procedure.
b)  **Referral to the Public Prosecutor’s Office** if, despite initially finding no indication that the facts may constitute a crime, this determination changes over the course of the investigation. If the crime affects the financial interests of the European Union, the case will be referred to the European Public Prosecutor’s Office.
c)  **Transfer of all actions to the competent authority**, in accordance with the provisions of this Procedure.
d)  **Initiation of disciplinary proceedings**.

Upon conclusion of all actions, the System Administrator will retain information related to the report within the whistleblowing channel software, which will contain:

a)  Date of receipt.
b)  Classification of the report.
c)  Closure date.
d)  Actions taken.

This record will not be public, and only upon a reasoned request from the competent judicial authority, within the framework of a judicial proceeding and under its supervision, may partial or full access to the content of the record be granted.

Personal data related to received reports and internal investigations referred to in the preceding section will only be retained for as long as necessary and proportionate to comply with applicable legislation. In no case will data be retained for more than ten years.

If the investigation concludes with verified proof that the reported facts are true and involve irregular or unlawful conduct, the accused may be subject to sanctions in accordance with the applicable Collective Bargaining Agreement, labour legislation, and other civil and commercial obligations held by Company staff, executive personnel, or the Board of Directors.

The Company may also refer the matter to the relevant judicial authorities if deemed necessary.

If the accused is a third party with no employment relationship with the Company (supplier, commercial agent, or business partner), applicable sanctions will be limited to the commercial sphere (limitation of actions, unilateral termination of the contract by the Company), without prejudice to the aforementioned communication to the competent judicial authorities.

nodes_flat.title: 6.11. Reporting of False or Malicious Claims
nodes_flat.summary: The Company's whistleblowing channel must be used responsibly. Reporting false facts with malicious intent or in bad faith is a serious breach of trust. This can lead to disciplinary action for employees (per Collective Bargaining Agreement) or commercial measures for other stakeholders. If a report is found to be manifestly false and malicious, it's filed. Then, the HR Department proposes disciplinary measures, and a written sanction proposal goes to the General HR Directorate or Board of Directors to determine action against the 'malicious informant'.
nodes_flat.content:
The Company’s whistleblowing channel must be used responsibly and appropriately. Reporting false facts with a malicious and morally dishonest attitude constitutes a breach of the good faith that should govern professional and employment relationships within the Company and may lead to disciplinary action in accordance with the applicable Collective Bargaining Agreement or, for other stakeholders, commercial measures.

If, after proper analysis, it is concluded that the reported facts are manifestly false and that the report was submitted with malicious intent and in bad faith: (i) the report will be filed, (ii) the matter will be referred to the Human Resources Department to propose disciplinary measures in accordance with the applicable Collective Bargaining Agreement, and (iii) a written sanction proposal will be submitted to the General Human Resources Directorate or, where applicable, to the Board of Directors, which will determine the disciplinary action to be applied to the malicious informant.

nodes_flat.title: 6.12. Personal Data Protection
nodes_flat.summary: Personal data handling follows Organic Law 3/2018, Article 11, unless it concerns anti-money laundering, counter-terrorist financing, or sanctions, in which case Law 10/2010 applies. The person reported on will be informed of the estimated processing time. The Company guarantees the informant's confidentiality. The rights of the person reported on are limited to their own personal data and will never include details about who made the report. If notifying the subject risks the informant's identity, the informant's confidentiality prevails, as per Law 2/2023, Article 31.2. Further information is available in the privacy policy.
nodes_flat.content:
The processing of personal data arising from the application of this Procedure will be governed by the provisions of Article 11 of Organic Law 3/2018 of 5 December, on Personal Data Protection and the Guarantee of Digital Rights, unless the report is related to matters of anti-money laundering, counter-terrorist financing, and/or sanctions, in which case the provisions of Law 10/2010 of 28 April and its implementing regulations on anti-money laundering, counter-terrorist financing, and specific sanctions will apply. Lastly, the person who is the subject of the report will be informed of the estimated time for processing the report.

In any case, the Company, as a guarantee of confidentiality for the informant, confirms that, generally, the exercise of rights by the person under investigation may be limited due to the specific nature of the report and may only be exercised regarding their own personal data that is being processed. Under no circumstances will the rights exercised by the person under investigation include data relating to the informant. If it is not possible to adopt measures to safeguard the informant’s confidentiality when notifying the person under investigation, the informant's right to confidentiality will prevail in accordance with Article 31.2 of Law 2/2023 of 20 February, which regulates the protection of individuals who report regulatory breaches and fights against corruption.

Further information is available in our privacy policy at [https://imaginemontessori.es/politica-de-privacidad/](https://imaginemontessori.es/politica-de-privacidad/).

nodes_flat.title: 6.13. Annex
nodes_flat.summary: Honey, your child's whistleblowing channel is for serious concerns like illegal activities, fraud, and safety violations. This includes financial crimes like bribery (improperly influencing actions) and money laundering (hiding crime money). They can report breaches of Information Security, Privacy, and Confidentiality (e.g., sharing company secrets, employee data) or Cybersecurity risks (e.g., M&A plan leaks, identity theft). Workplace issues cover discrimination (gender, race, age, etc.), Sexual/Gender-Based Harassment (unwanted sexual conduct creating a hostile environment), and Workplace Harassment (systematic hostile treatment). Regulatory non-compliance includes Accounting and Auditing issues (messing financial records), Non-Compliance with Corporate Behaviours (taking gifts), Serious Acts of Disrespect, or ignoring Health and Safety Risks. Any internal or labor regulation breach is reportable. There's also an "Other Reportable Incidents" category, and the Whistleblowing Channel Protocol handles false or bad faith reports.

nodes_flat.title: Introduction
nodes_flat.summary: "What kind of issues can my child report through this 'whistleblowing channel'? Is it for serious concerns like illegal activities, fraud, or safety violations? Or also for things like harassment, discrimination, or other unethical behavior and misconduct? I want them to understand when it's important to speak up about wrongdoing."

nodes_flat.title: Financial and Security Crimes
nodes_flat.summary: Financial crimes include bribery, which is giving someone an advantage to improperly influence their actions or reward them for bad behavior. It also covers money laundering, defined as handling assets known to come from crime—like converting, hiding, or using them—to conceal their illicit origin or help criminals. This includes participating in such acts too.
nodes_flat.content:
Corruption, Bribery, and Coercion                     | Bribery refers to acts that provide someone with a financial or other advantage to encourage that person to perform their duties or activities improperly or to reward them for having done so. This includes attempts to influence decision-makers by offering additional benefits beyond those legitimately allowed.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| Anti-Money Laundering and Counter-Terrorist Financing and Sanctions | Money laundering is defined as: i) Conversion or transfer of assets, knowing they derive from criminal activity or involvement in such activity, intending to conceal the illicit origin of the assets or to help individuals involved evade legal consequences; ii) Concealing or disguising the nature, origin, location, disposition, movement, or ownership of assets or rights to assets, knowing that these derive from criminal activity; iii) Acquisition, possession, or use of assets, knowing, at the time of receipt, that they derive from criminal activity or participation therein; iv) Participation in any of the above activities, association for such acts, attempts to commit them, or aiding, instigating, or advising others in carrying them out. |
|

nodes_flat.title: Information and Data Security
nodes_flat.summary: Information Security, Privacy, and Confidentiality mean never sharing company secrets with outsiders. This includes employee personal data (salaries, leaves), Company strategy, or business partner details. These obligations continue post-employment; using confidential info for personal gain is forbidden. Cybersecurity protects against risks like unauthorized access to M&A plans, intellectual property, or personal data theft. It also guards against financial fraud (e.g., payment diversion, identity theft) and business disruption (e.g., sabotage, denial of service).
nodes_flat.content:
Privacy / Information Security / Information Confidentiality | Privacy and information protection entail refraining from disclosing information to third parties, such as customer or employee personal information (salaries, leaves, etc.), Company security or strategy, and details about entities with which the Company maintains commercial relationships. These obligations continue even after employment ends, and confidential information is prohibited from being used for personal gain.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| Cybersecurity                                         | Cybersecurity risks include: i) unauthorised access or misuse of information or systems (e.g., personal data theft, M&A plans, intellectual property); ii) financial fraud and theft (e.g., diversion of payments, fund extraction, identity theft); iii) business disruption (e.g., sabotage, extortion, denial of service).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|

nodes_flat.title: Workplace Conduct and Discrimination
nodes_flat.summary: The company champions equal opportunities and non-discrimination in job access and career advancement. It strictly prohibits discrimination based on gender, sexual orientation, race, religion, disability, age, marital status, and other factors. Derogatory expressions related to sexual orientation or gender characteristics are also forbidden. Sexual or Gender-Based Harassment is defined as unwanted sexual conduct creating a discomforting, intimidating, offensive, or hostile work environment. Workplace Harassment means systematic hostile or vexatious treatment that creates an intimidating, offensive, or hostile environment. These rules ensure a fair and respectful atmosphere for all employees.
nodes_flat.content:
Equal Opportunities and Non-Discrimination            | Conduct not aligned with the Company’s principle of equal opportunities in job access and career advancement, ensuring the absence of discrimination based on gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, sexual characteristics, race, religion, disability, origin, marital status, age, or social status. Also includes derogatory expressions based on sexual orientation, gender identity, or gender characteristics in public or private service provision.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| Sexual or Gender-Based Harassment                     | Disrespectful or unwanted conduct of a sexual nature that causes discomfort and creates an intimidating, offensive, or hostile work environment.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| Workplace Harassment                                  | Systematic hostile or vexatious treatment in the workplace that creates an intimidating, offensive, or hostile environment.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
|

nodes_flat.title: Regulatory and Policy Non-Compliance
nodes_flat.summary: Honey, this part is about when people don't follow the rules at work. It covers big no-nos like 'Accounting and Auditing' issues – that's messing with financial records, fake numbers, or trying to influence the auditors. Then there's 'Non-Compliance with Corporate Behaviours,' which means unprofessional conduct, like a boss or co-worker taking gifts or benefits for themselves, compromising their fairness. 'Serious Acts of Disrespect' are also a problem. And it includes not following any 'Internal or Labour Regulations,' even those in our collective agreement, or ignoring 'Health and Safety Risks' policies. It's basically about breaking any important company or legal rule.
nodes_flat.content:
Accounting and Auditing                               | Tampering or falsification of financial information, inaccuracies in financial statements, intentional misinformation, undue influence on auditors, questionable accounting, auditing, or internal financial control practices.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| Non-Compliance with Corporate Behaviours              | Unprofessional conduct by co-workers or managers that does not align with the Company’s corporate behaviours. Instances where a professional abuses their role by offering, giving, promising, requesting, or accepting any form of gift, benefit, consideration, or invitation to obtain a personal advantage for themselves or a third party, compromising their impartiality.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| Serious Acts of Disrespect                            | Acts involving serious disrespect by colleagues or managers in the workplace.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| Non-Compliance with Internal or Labour Regulations    | Any non-compliance with (legal or conventional) regulations, policies, or internal Company procedures involving a breach of a labour obligation, including those categorised in the current collective agreement.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| Health and Safety Risks                               | Any non-compliance with regulations related to established policies within the scope of Business Safety and Hygiene.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|

nodes_flat.title: Other Reportable Incidents
nodes_flat.summary: The "Other Reportable Incidents" category serves as a catch-all for events not classified in the main annex list. A crucial rule is that the Whistleblowing Channel Protocol specifically governs the handling of reports determined to be false or made in bad faith.
nodes_flat.content: Other                                                 | Section for reporting any event that cannot be classified within the present annex list. The Whistleblowing Channel Protocol stipulates the handling of reports considered false or made in bad faith.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |

nodes_flat.title: 6.14. Monitoring and Review
nodes_flat.summary: The Imagine Leadership Team, part of School Leadership, is responsible for monitoring and reviewing this policy. This process occurs biennially, meaning every two years.
nodes_flat.content: **School Leadership**: The Imagine Leadership Team is responsible for monitoring and reviewing the policy biennially.

nodes_flat.title: 6.15. Reference Documents
nodes_flat.summary: Key rulebooks cited protect individuals and ensure fair practices. Whistleblower protection is covered by EU Directive 2019/1937 and Spanish Law 2/2023. Personal data privacy is guaranteed by GDPR 2016/679, Organic Law 3/2018, and Constitution Art. 18.1. Law 10/2010 fights money laundering. Equality for all, including gender (Organic Law 3/2007), sexual freedom (Organic Law 10/2022), and LGBT+ rights (Law 4/2023), is mandated. Harassment, defined by EU Directive 2002/73/C and Law 63/2003, is prohibited, as per Workers' Statute Art. 4.2.e. Workplace safety and health (WHO definition, Prevention Law Arts. 14, 15) are also detailed. Financial market rules (RDL 4/2015, Law 35/2003) and the Penal Code (Organic Law 10/1995) complete the framework.
nodes_flat.content:
*   **DIRECTIVE (EU) 2019/1937 OF THE EUROPEAN PARLIAMENT AND OF THE COUNCIL** of 23 October 2019 on the protection of persons who report breaches of Union law.
*   **Law 10/2010, of 28 April on Anti-Money Laundering**.
*   **Organic Law 3/2018, of 5 December** on the Protection of Personal Data.
*   **Law 2/2023, of 20 February on Whistleblower Protection**.
*   **Law 35/2003, of 4 November, on Collective Investment Institutions**.
*   **Organic Law 10/1995, of 23 November, the Penal Code**.
*   **Royal Legislative Decree 4/2015, of 23 October**, approving the revised text of the Securities Market Act.
*   **REGULATION (EU) 2016/679 OF THE EUROPEAN PARLIAMENT AND OF THE COUNCIL** of 27 April 2016 on the protection of natural persons concerning personal data processing and on the free movement of such data (General Data Protection Regulation).
*   **Organic Law 10/2022, of 6 September on comprehensive sexual freedom assurance**.
*   **Organic Law 3/2007, of 22 March for effective equality between men and women**.
*   **Article 18.1 of the Spanish Constitution**: “The right to honour, personal and family privacy, and one’s own image is guaranteed.”
*   **Article 4.2.e) of the Workers’ Statute**: “Workers are entitled to respect for their privacy and consideration of their dignity, including protection from verbal and physical harassment of a sexual nature and harassment based on racial or ethnic origin, religion or beliefs, disability, age, or sexual orientation.”
*   **European Parliament, Article 2 of Directive 2002/73/C of 23 September 2003**, and **Law 63/2003, of 30 December**, on fiscal, administrative, and social order measures, Article 28.2, which introduce the concept of harassment. This law defines harassment as “any unwanted conduct related to racial or ethnic origin, religion or beliefs, disabilities, age, or sexual orientation of a person, aimed at or resulting in violating dignity and creating an intimidating, humiliating, or offensive environment.”
*   **World Health Organization Constitution**: (WHO): “Health is a state of complete physical, mental, and social well-being, and not merely the absence of disease or infirmity.”
*   **Art. 14 of the Occupational Health and Safety Law, right to protection from occupational risks**: “Workers have the right to effective protection in matters of safety and health at work.”
*   **Art. 15 of the Prevention Law, principles of preventive action**: “The employer will apply measures that integrate the general duty of Prevention.”
*   **Law 4/2023 for real and effective equality of trans individuals and for the guarantee of LGBT+ rights**.

children.title: 1. Fire and Emergency Policy
children.summary: Our Fire & Emergency Policy prioritizes your child's safety. We hold termly emergency drills and annual Risk Assessments. Health & Safety Officers and an External Technician identify hazards. Our Leadership Team validates the Emergency Plan, ensuring clear evacuation procedures for everyone, including families, visitors, and contractors. If you're on-site during an evacuation, please follow staff instructions immediately to the Fire Assembly Point. All visitors sign in/out, wear a green lanyard, and are escorted by staff. Evacuation routes are always clear. Fire alarms are regularly tested; firefighting equipment annually. The Leadership Team reviews this policy biennially (every two years). We use Contractor Hazard Exchange Forms for outside workers.

children.title: 1.1. Scope
children.content:
The safety of the children and staff at Imagine Montessori School is paramount. Therefore, the risk assessment ensures the safety of everyone. There is an emergency drill carried out every term. A risk assessment is made annually or when there is a significant change or new risk to consider.

**The Health and Safety Officers:**

*   Identify the hazard, record significant findings and assess the risks
*   Review the existing control measures
*   Where necessary, provide additional control measures
*   Carry out risk assessments

**The External Technician in Emergency Risk Assessment is responsible for:**

*   Identification of hazards
*   Detect who might be harmed and how
*   Evaluate the risks, deciding whether existing controls are adequate, or the school needs to do more
*   Record findings
*   Review and revise annually

These responsible agents collaborate to prepare an emergency plan in accordance with the risks identified. The emergency plan is validated with the Leadership Team and explained and practised with the whole Imagine Team.
children.summary: Imagine Montessori prioritizes child and staff safety. An emergency drill is carried out every term. Risk assessments are done annually or when significant changes occur. Health and Safety Officers identify hazards, assess risks, and manage control measures. An External Technician in Emergency Risk Assessment also identifies hazards, evaluates risks, and records findings annually. These roles collaborate to create an emergency plan, validated by the Leadership Team and practiced with the whole Imagine Team.

children.title: 1.2. Terminology
children.content:
Fire and Emergency Policy: The policy established to ensure the safety of individuals in the building in the event of a fire or other emergency.

Risk Assessment: The process of identifying and evaluating risks to ensure a safe environment.

Health and Safety Officers: Personnel responsible for identifying hazards, implementing control measures, and overseeing safety procedures.

External Technician in Emergency Risk Assessment: An external specialist responsible for evaluating emergency risks and providing expert guidance.

Emergency Plan: A document outlining the procedures to be followed during an emergency to ensure the safety of everyone on the premises.

Fire Assembly Point: A designated area where individuals must gather after evacuating the building.

Contractor Hazard Exchange Form/Checklist: A tool used to assess and mitigate specific risks associated with contractors working on the premises.
children.summary: Understanding safety terms is crucial. A Fire and Emergency Policy outlines rules for building safety during emergencies. Risk Assessment identifies and evaluates dangers. Health and Safety Officers manage hazards and oversee procedures. An External Technician is an outside expert for emergency risk evaluation. The Emergency Plan details procedures for everyone's safety. The Fire Assembly Point is the designated gathering spot after evacuation. A Contractor Hazard Exchange Form manages risks from outside workers.

children.title: 1.3. Policy Statement and Principles
children.content:
Imagine Montessori School is committed to ensuring the safety of all children, staff, and visitors on campus. This commitment is reflected in the implementation of policies and practices that:

1.  Ensure continuous risk assessments to identify hazards and mitigate them effectively.
2.  Promote regular drills and staff training to guarantee swift and organised responses in case of emergencies.
3.  Provide clear information and evacuation procedures to all parties involved, including families, visitors, and contractors.
4.  Maintain accessible evacuation routes and ensure emergency equipment is in optimal condition through regular inspections.

These principles are designed to ensure a safe and orderly environment during any emergency.
children.summary: Imagine Montessori is committed to safety for all (children, staff, visitors). Our policy includes continuous risk assessments to mitigate hazards, regular drills & staff training for swift emergency responses, and clear evacuation procedures shared with families, visitors, & contractors. We maintain accessible evacuation routes and optimal emergency equipment through regular inspections, ensuring a safe, orderly environment during any emergency.

children.title: 1.4. Emergency Evacuation Procedures for Families, Visitors, and Volunteers
children.content: In case any family, visitors or volunteers are on the school premises when an emergency evacuation takes place, please follow the instructions of the member of staff in charge.
children.summary: If you, as a parent, visitor, or volunteer, are on school premises during an emergency evacuation, the key rule is to immediately follow all instructions from the designated staff member in charge. This ensures everyone's safety during the "emergency evacuation procedure."

children.title: 1.5. Staff absences
children.content: Should any member of staff be absent, their duties in evacuating the children in their care from the building will be undertaken by the teacher or adult in charge of the class. Supply staff will also be required to sweep any areas they pass through for personnel as they exit the building.
children.summary: During staff absences, the teacher or adult in charge of the class will undertake duties for evacuating children. Supply staff are also required to sweep any areas they pass through for personnel as they exit the building during an evacuation.

children.title: 1.6. Visitor Registration and Safety Procedures
children.content:
**Visitor Registration**

All visitors and contractors must report to Reception, signing in upon arrival and signing out before leaving the premises. Once signed in, they will receive a green lanyard, which must be worn throughout their visit. During a fire evacuation, the staff member hosting the visitor is responsible for ensuring they are escorted to the designated fire assembly point.

**Contractors Safety Procedures**

For contractors, there is a distinction between recurrent and non-recurrent roles:

*   **Recurrent contractors (e.g., cleaning or kitchen staff)** have their adapted Fire and Emergency Protocol, and receive basic fire safety training to ensure they are prepared for emergencies, even outside regular school hours. They are informed of the action to be taken on hearing the fire alarm or discovering a fire, the evacuation procedures including means of escape, location of the fire assembly points and name of the person in charge of evacuation procedures, and the location of firefighting equipment and fire alarm call points in relation to the area of their work.
*   **Non-recurrent contractors** receive a brief summary of emergency procedures upon arrival, including the location of emergency exits and the fire assembly point. In the event of an evacuation, they are instructed to join the nearest evacuation team and follow their guidance to ensure a safe exit.

This approach ensures all individuals on the premises are informed and capable of responding appropriately in an emergency.
children.summary: All visitors and contractors must sign in at Reception, wear a green lanyard, and sign out upon leaving. During a fire evacuation, host staff escort visitors to the fire assembly point. Recurrent contractors (e.g., cleaning) have adapted fire protocols and training, covering evacuation, assembly points, and equipment. Non-recurrent contractors receive a brief on emergency exits and assembly points upon arrival, joining an evacuation team if needed. This ensures everyone's safety on premises.

children.title: 1.7. Evacuation routes
children.content: Evacuation routes will be kept free from obstruction and adequately and clearly marked.
children.summary: Emergency evacuation routes will always be kept clear of any obstructions. These critical pathways will also be adequately and clearly marked, ensuring everyone can easily find their way out safely during an emergency.

children.title: Fire Safety Equipment Testing
children.content:
The fire alarms are regularly tested by a certified agent.

Firefighting equipment will be examined and tested at least once a year by a competent service engineer.
children.summary: For safety, fire alarms are regularly tested by a certified agent. All firefighting equipment, like extinguishers, is examined and tested at least once a year by a competent service engineer. This ensures all gear is ready and compliant.

children.title: 1.10. Monitoring and Review
children.content: The Imagine Leadership Team is responsible for monitoring and reviewing the policy biennially.
children.summary: Who keeps an eye on this policy? The Imagine Leadership Team. They're in charge of monitoring and reviewing it. This happens biennially, so you can expect it to be checked and updated every two years.

children.title: 1.11. Reference Documents
children.content:
*   Workplace Health and Safety Regulations.
*   Risk Assessment and Emergency Planning Guide for Educational Institutions.
*   Fire Evacuation and Prevention Regulations from the Department for Education and Safety.
*   Contractor Hazard Exchange Forms and Checklists.
*   Imagine Montessori School Emergency Procedures Protocol.
children.summary: Imagine Montessori School's safety protocols are guided by Workplace Health and Safety Regulations, a Risk Assessment and Emergency Planning Guide for Educational Institutions, and Fire Evacuation and Prevention Regulations from the Department for Education and Safety. We manage contractor safety using Hazard Exchange Forms and Checklists. Our Imagine Montessori School Emergency Procedures Protocol details all school-specific emergency responses, ensuring student well-being.

children.title: 2. Health & Safety Policy
children.summary: Imagine Montessori's H&S policy ensures a safe environment, adhering to Law 31/1995 and Decreto 253/2019. Responsibility is shared: HR (Workplace Safety), Facility Manager (Buildings), Head Teachers/Admin (daily measures). Key terms: Risk Assessment (identifying hazards), First Aider (trained staff). Maintenance performs daily safety checks; the policy is reviewed biennially. All staff receive H&S training (risk assessment, evacuation, first-aid) and are qualified first-aiders, trained annually, exceeding ratios. The Secretary handles minor accidents; trained first-aiders act for emergencies. Parents are notified via Phidias and sign accident reports. Medication is administered with signed consent, witnessed, and recorded. A wasp sting protocol details actions for mild vs. severe reactions (anaphylaxis, call 112, EpiPen if prescribed). Breaches are documented via a 'Record of Concern.' The school upholds a 'duty of care' like a 'prudent parent.'

children.title: 2.1. Scope
children.content:
Children should have the opportunity to engage in a broad variety of activities. Health and safety measures should support this goal, ensuring their well-being without hindering their participation. It is crucial for children to learn to recognise and manage risks as part of their everyday life. When assessing and managing risks, common sense should be applied, and health and safety procedures should always be proportionate to the risks associated with each activity. Staff should receive appropriate training to ensure they can protect themselves and the children, and effectively manage any potential risks.

**Responsibility of the school**

The employer is required to have a health and safety policy in place and ensure the necessary measures are implemented to enforce it. This is regulated under the Law 31/1995 on the *Prevention of Occupational Risks*. Employers are obligated to assess the risks associated with all activities, adopt measures to manage those risks, and communicate these measures to their employees. Furthermore, the organisation of preventive activities is governed by the Regulation of Prevention Services (*Royal Decree 39/1997*).

In practice, employers may delegate specific health and safety tasks to designated individuals. However, ultimate responsibility always remains with the employer, regardless of who carries out the tasks.

It is therefore essential for the employer to maintain a clear system of documentation and monitoring to ensure there is an audit trail. This should specify who is responsible for each task and confirm that these tasks are being performed appropriately.

**Responsibility of the employees**

At Imagine Montessori School, responsibility for health and safety is shared across different roles. To manage health and safety effectively, the following responsibilities are defined:

*   **Policy Definition and Oversight:** Specific areas are assigned to designated individuals, such as the Responsibility for Workplace Safety (HR Responsible) and oversight of Buildings and Facilities (Facility Manager).
*   **Implementation:** The practical application of health and safety measures is managed by the Head Teachers and the Head of Administration within their respective domains.

This collaborative structure ensures that all areas of the school’s operations maintain the highest standards of safety and compliance.
children.summary: Children engage in varied activities, learning risk management. Our health & safety (H&S) procedures are proportionate. The school, as employer, must have an H&S policy (Law 31/1995, Prevention of Occupational Risks), assess/manage risks, and communicate measures. Preventive activities follow Royal Decree 39/1997. While H&S tasks can be delegated, ultimate responsibility always rests with the employer, requiring clear documentation and an audit trail. At Imagine Montessori School, H&S is shared: the HR Responsible handles Workplace Safety, the Facility Manager oversees Buildings/Facilities, and Head Teachers/Head of Administration implement practical H&S measures daily.

children.title: 2.2. Terminology
children.content:
Risk Assessment: The process of identifying hazards, evaluating risks, and implementing measures to control them.

First Aider: A staff member trained and certified to provide immediate medical assistance.
children.summary: Key terms include Risk Assessment, a process to identify hazards, evaluate risks, and implement controls. A First Aider is a trained, certified staff member providing immediate medical assistance.

children.title: 2.3. Policy Statement and Principles
children.summary: Imagine Montessori commits to a safe, healthy environment for all. Our Maintenance Team performs daily safety checks (indoors/outdoors) for damage (e.g., fallen tiles), hazards (glass, animal faeces, dangerous plants), clear entrances/exits, and unlocked emergency exits. Health and safety measures are reviewed annually; electrical/gas equipment is inspected, with service histories logged, and strict hygiene maintained. All staff receive H&S training, covering risk assessment, emergency evacuation, first-aid, and awareness of illness signs like meningitis, hepatitis B, AIDS. Maintenance staff are trained in utility shut-offs. Contractors are vetted for H&S awareness and track record. The main door is always locked; two doors are supervised for children's arrival/departure. Slips/trips are prevented, with Head Teachers conducting periodic risk assessments. All accidents, including RIDDOR 1995 reportable ones, are recorded. Fire safety, off-site visits, and staff violence are detailed in separate policies.

children.title: General Policy Statement
children.content:
**A. A general statement of policy**

Imagine Montessori is committed to providing a safe and healthy work environment for all its employees, children and visitors.

**
children.summary: Imagine Montessori's policy is a firm commitment to a safe and healthy environment for all. This includes our employees, your children, and every visitor on site.

children.title: Daily Safety Checks and Responsibilities
children.content:
B. Delegation of duties as allocated tasks**

*   The general safety of the building, indoors and outdoors, is checked daily by the Maintenance Team. This includes checking the general condition of the building, e.g. fallen tiles. In finding any damage, the Facilities Manager of the school will be notified immediately. The area will be made safe. If there is any broken glass, litter, cans, ring pulls as well as animal faeces all should be cleared.
*   Check all entrances and exits are clear and in working order.
*   Unlock and check all emergency exits, garden fences and gates, locks and bolts on doors.
*   Check that all climbing equipment is safe. Check the garden for fungi and unwanted dangerous plants.
*   Check that all emergency lighting is in working order, that light switches are safe, and that all areas are adequately lit.
*   Other duties all members of staff are expected to be aware of and uphold.

**
children.summary: Daily safety checks are performed by the Maintenance Team. They inspect the building (indoors/outdoors) for damage like fallen tiles, notifying the Facilities Manager immediately and making areas safe. They clear hazards (glass, litter, animal faeces). Checks include clear entrances/exits, unlocked emergency exits, safe climbing equipment, and garden hazards (fungi, dangerous plants). Emergency lighting and general illumination are also checked. All staff uphold safety duties.

children.title: Monitoring and Review of Health and Safety Measures
children.content:
C. Arrangements made to put in place, monitor and review measures necessary to reach satisfactory health and safety standards:**

All arrangements are monitored and reviewed annually. All electrical and gas equipment is to be maintained and subject to periodical inspections. The service histories should be recorded in a logbook.

Faults and repairs are to be logged. For example, a piece of equipment that is damaged should be labelled and removed until it can be either repaired or discarded. Strict hygiene standards are set and maintained.

**
children.summary: Health and safety measures are reviewed annually. All electrical and gas equipment is maintained and periodically inspected, with service histories recorded in a logbook. Faults and repairs are logged; damaged items are labelled, removed, and then repaired or discarded. Strict hygiene standards are always maintained.

children.title: Staff Training in Health and Safety
children.content:
D. Training of staff in health and safety including competence in risk assessment**

All members of staff should have the school Health and Safety procedures clearly explained to them as part of the induction process and are given the Health and Safety policies in their induction pack. This includes the school hygiene regime. As part of a rolling programme, staff are trained in Health and Safety including competence in risk assessment.

All new staff should be trained in the evacuation procedures in the event of a fire or other emergency. All members of staff should have first-aid training (see first-aid policy). All staff are to be kept aware of signs of illness such as meningitis, hepatitis B, AIDS. All maintenance staff must know where to turn the water and electricity supplies off and must be trained to use electrical equipment correctly and safely. Use regular staff meetings to focus on different health and safety issues such as keeping up to date on tetanus vaccinations.

**E. Off-site visits including school-led adventure activities**

See separate policy: Trips and Outings Policy

**
children.summary: All staff receive health & safety training, including risk assessment competence, during induction and a rolling program. This covers school hygiene, emergency evacuation, and first-aid. Staff are also made aware of illness signs like meningitis, hepatitis B, AIDS. Maintenance staff are trained in utility shut-offs and safe electrical equipment use. Regular meetings address H&S, e.g., tetanus vaccinations. Off-site visits are detailed in a separate Trips and Outings Policy.

children.title: Contractor Selection and Control
children.content:
F. Selecting and controlling contractors**

The school will use these indicators to select and control contractors: past performance, work undertaken in other schools, membership of trade bodies, accreditation of trade bodies, general health and safety awareness, organisation and arrangements for health and safety and commitment to recognised codes of practice.

**
children.summary: To select and manage contractors, the school checks their track record, experience with other schools, and if they belong to accredited trade bodies. Crucially, we assess their health and safety awareness, safety arrangements, and commitment to industry codes. This ensures qualified, safe professionals work on school premises.

children.title: Workplace Safety and Emergency Procedures
children.content:
H. School Security**

This is covered in the check of the inside and external building by the Maintenance Team. The main door is always locked. The doors that are used to access the outside areas are only opened when such areas are under supervision. At the start and end of the school day, two doors are used for children to arrive or depart, and both are continuously supervised by Imagine’s staff. All other doors remain secure and are not used during these times.

**I. Occupational health services and work-related stress**

The school will identify all workplace stressors and conduct risk assessments to eliminate stress or control the risks from stress. These risk assessments will be regularly reviewed.

**J. Consultation arrangements with staff**

The school shall consult its employees in good time on matters relating to their health and safety at work regarding the introduction of any measure in the school that will affect the health and safety of the employees; any health and safety information they are required to provide to those employees by or under the relevant statutory provisions.

**K. Workplace safety for teachers, pupils and visitors**

The school through all its health and safety regulations ensures the workplace is safe for all.

**L. Violence to staff**

See separate policy: Behaviour Policy (Document “5. Code of Conduct”)

**M. Slips and trips:**

Everyone in the school has a part to play when it comes to preventing slips and trips: clear spillages immediately, remove any obstructions and wear sensible shoes. Periodically, the Head Teachers develop risk assessments.

**N. On site vehicle movements:**

Not applicable

**
children.summary: School security involves daily Maintenance Team checks. The main door is always locked. Two doors are continuously supervised by Imagine’s staff for children's arrival/departure; other doors stay secure. Workplace safety for children, staff, and visitors is ensured by school regulations. Staff are consulted on health/safety. Workplace stress is managed via risk assessments. Slips/trips are prevented by clearing spills, removing obstructions, and wearing sensible shoes; Head Teachers conduct periodic risk assessments. Violence to staff is covered in the Behaviour Policy (Document "5. Code of Conduct").

children.title: Accident Reporting and Maintenance
children.content:
O. Maintenance and when necessary, examination and test of plant and equipment such as electrical equipment, local exhaust ventilation, pressure systems, gas appliances, lifting equipment and glazing safety**

The school would identify the hazard or suspected hazard and use an external contractor.

**P. Recording and reporting accidents to staff, pupils and visitors - including those reportable under the Reporting of Injuries, Diseases and Dangerous Occurrences Regulations 1995 (RIDDOR).**

The school would record any of the above.

**Q. Fire safety, including testing of alarms and evacuation procedures**

See separate policy: Fire Safety Policy

**R. Dealing with health and safety emergencies- procedures and contacts**

By having policies, guidance and training for all the above, the school can deal with any emergencies using the correct procedures and contacts.
children.summary: The school maintains safety by using external contractors for regular examination and testing of equipment like electrical systems, gas appliances, and lifting gear, addressing identified hazards. All accidents involving staff, pupils, and visitors are recorded and reported, including those reportable under RIDDOR 1995. Fire safety, including alarm testing and evacuation procedures, is detailed in a separate Fire Safety Policy. Health and safety emergencies are managed through established policies, guidance, and training.

children.title: 2.4. First aid
children.content:
All Imagine’s staff have qualified first-aider status and are trained on an annual recurrent basis. Therefore, we have well above the required ratio of first aiders to pupils. For minor accidents, they will refer any accidents or any other medical conditions to the designated member of staff: Secretary. For medical emergencies, the person who finds the child/adult will deal with it immediately. Only trained first aiders administer first aid.

There is always more than one designated first-aider on the site of the school. All first aiders attend training courses biannually. First aid equipment is in the designated first aid cupboard, which is kept in the admin staff room and bathroom.

All accidents and other medical emergencies should be recorded appropriately in an accident report form. Parents /guardians should be notified and asked to sign the record sheet and a record kept. In the case of students using our bus service, parents will be notified by Phidias and we expect an acknowledgment. The accident report will be kept both on paper and in digital format at the Secretary.

Based on the information from accident reports, risk analyses are periodically conducted using multivariable analysis to implement mitigation actions for recurring potential risks.

If the accident is serious, the head teacher should be made aware and immediate action taken to ensure the location of the accident is still safe to use. Necessary action should be taken and where possible details recorded for an accident investigation. If members of the public are involved, names and addresses should be taken (including any witnesses).

Members of staff should be made aware of any hazards in the subjects that they teach or the activities that they supervise and should have appropriate training and access to first aid kits.

Pupils with medical conditions, such as epilepsy, asthma or severe allergies should be identified. All staff should be aware of their conditions and alert to the need for prompt action. The Secretary administers medication and is witnessed by another member of staff. A written record is kept and signed by the two members of staff. Medication is kept in the office or the refrigerator in Reception. A signed consent form is needed from the parent /guardian before prescribed medication is administered.

Staff should be aware of what levels of injury can be treated on-site and what levels automatically trigger an emergency ambulance call or visit to the hospital.

There are hygiene procedures in place in case of spillage of blood or body fluid: Employees must wear disposable gloves and aprons when cleaning any bodily fluids (spillages of blood, vomit, urine and excreta). Other persons should be kept away from the contamination until it is effectively dealt with.

The following action should be taken in the event of spillages:

*   Soak up the spillages using paper towels
*   Clean the area with hot water containing soap or detergent
*   Clean the area with a bleach or disinfectant solution - use as directed by the manufacturer.
*   'Solids’ should be carefully flushed down the toilet. Dressings, gloves, and aprons should be carefully disposed of in sealed plastic bags as clinical waste.
children.summary: All Imagine staff are qualified first-aiders, trained annually, well above the required ratio. For minor accidents, the Secretary is the designated staff member. For medical emergencies, trained first-aiders act immediately. First aid equipment is in the designated cupboard in the admin staff room and bathroom.
